purpose: Apply B51's fix: build a GRADEABLE orthogonal-directions candidate. B51 proved the B42 chunked forward is grader-safe and localized the S4/S5 grader 'Evaluation error' to the FRAME ops fnp.concatenate and/or fnp.where. This candidate keeps B49's QR-free Gram-Schmidt frame and B42's forward but removes both frame suspects.
changes_vs_B49_GS: fnp.concatenate(blocks)/u_all/reshape -> forward each width-row orbit block DIRECTLY (each block a natural chunk; summing per-block == summing one concatenated batch). No fnp.concatenate, no fnp.reshape.
dropped the +100 iid tail so all 25 blocks are equal-size -> 6400 directions (vs 6500); negligible statistical cost.
op_set: Only ops S3/B42 graded successfully: standard_normal, astype, matmul, maximum, fnp.sum(axis=0,dtype=float64), stack, linalg.norm, comparison(>=), scalar arithmetic. NO fnp.concatenate / fnp.where / fnp.linalg.qr / fnp.reshape.

mini_100.note: MSE +1.5% vs B46 (5.017e-06) from 6400 vs 6500 samples -- orthogonal benefit preserved

vs_gs_and_b46: B53 adjusted Full -16.5% (gradeable) vs GS/B50 -20.1% (ungradeable, failed grading S5) vs B46 ~-23% (ungradeable, failed grading S4). The ~3.6% gap to GS is the dropped iid rows + block-wise forward overhead (multiplier 0.1158 vs GS 0.1118). B53 is the best SUBMITTABLE candidate: it trades a few % of margin for using only the ops B51 confirmed grade.
verdict: PASS + GRADEABLE. B53's Full gate satisfies all AGENTS.md step-7 GATE prerequisites (paired Full CI entirely below zero at -4.2/-5.3 sigma; -16.5% over last_submitted >> 5% bar; zero failures) using ONLY grader-safe ops. It is the gradeable realization of the orthogonal win.
next_action: Submit B53 -- a FRESH lead/user decision (B53 is a new candidate; the S5 user ruling covered only the GS blob f54b23b; 3 attempts used today of 10). This is the decisive test of B51's frame-op localization: if B53 GRADES (as its op-set predicts, since B51 proved the forward safe and B53 removed both frame suspects), it realizes a ~-16% improvement over the S3 leaderboard 6.6845e-07 -- the first orthogonal-win submission that grades. If it STILL fails, the remaining suspect is broadcast-multiply / a grader resource limit (escalate). I did NOT submit (no authorization for B53).
